welcomeMessage.en: Today, we are looking forward to the future.
We'll discuss personal goals, lifestyle changes, and New Year's resolutions.
welcomeMessage.zh: 我们将讨论个人目标、生活方式的改变以及新年决心。

welcomeMessage.en: Get ready to share your biggest ambitions and how you plan to achieve them!
welcomeMessage.zh: 准备好分享你最远大的抱负以及你打算如何实现它们吧！

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What is your resolution for this year? Have you been able to keep it?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你今年的决心/计划是什么？你能坚持下来吗？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: When it comes to studying or working, are you a very strict planner, or do you prefer to take things one day at a time?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 在学习或工作方面，你是一个非常严格的计划者，还是更喜欢顺其自然/走一步看一步？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Who is the most inspiring person you know, and why?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你认识的最鼓舞人心的人是谁？为什么？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: At what age do people usually move out of their parents' house in your culture?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 在你的文化中，人们通常在几岁搬出父母的家？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: If you could go to any amusement park in the world right now, what is the first ride you would go on?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 如果你现在可以去世界上任何一个游乐园，你会玩的第一个项目是什么？

vocabulary.definition: A firm decision to do or not to do something.
vocabulary.example: My New Year's resolution is to read one book every month.

vocabulary.definition: To honor or fulfill a commitment.
vocabulary.example: It is easy to make a resolution, but it is very hard to keep it all year.

vocabulary.definition: Demanding that rules concerning behavior are obeyed and observed.
vocabulary.example: She has a very strict diet plan because she wants to get healthier.

vocabulary.word: Take things one day at a time
vocabulary.definition: To deal with things as they happen, and not to make plans or to worry about the future.
vocabulary.translation: 一步一个脚印 / 顺其自然地过好每一天
vocabulary.example: Don't worry too much about the future; just take things one day at a time.

vocabulary.definition: A detailed proposal for doing or achieving something.
vocabulary.example: We need to make a detailed plan before we go on our road trip.
He goes to the gym and eats a lot of protein because he wants to gain weight.

vocabulary.definition: Having the effect of inspiring someone.
vocabulary.translation: 鼓舞人心的 / 启发人的
vocabulary.example: Listening to his success story was deeply inspiring.

vocabulary.definition: Leave one's previous home to live elsewhere.
vocabulary.example: After I graduate from university and get a job, I plan to move out.
You can go to the party as long as you finish your homework first.

vocabulary.definition: A large outdoor area with fairground rides, shows, and other entertainments.
vocabulary.example: Riding the rollercoasters at the amusement park is my favorite thing to do.

sessionSummary: Today's class was incredibly inspiring! We focused our discussion on the future, sharing our personal goals, lifestyle changes, and New Year's resolutions.

We talked about the different ways people approach their goals. Some people like to make a very strict plan and follow it perfectly, while others prefer not to stress too much and just take things one day at a time.

Everyone shared some wonderful personal goals. We talked about health and fitness, like trying to gain weight and build muscle. We also discussed big life steps, such as planning to move out of our parents' homes to live independently.
Of course, we didn't forget about having fun! We agreed that working hard is great, as long as we also make time for fun experiences, like planning a trip to an amusement park together.

keyVocabulary.example: "My New Year's resolution is to read one book every month."

keyVocabulary.spellOut: Keep a resolution, Keep a promise
keyVocabulary.example: "It is easy to make a resolution, but it is very hard to keep it all year."
"She has a very strict diet plan because she wants to get healthier."

keyVocabulary.word: Take things one day at a time
keyVocabulary.spellOut: Take things one day at a time
keyVocabulary.translation: 一步一个脚印 / 顺其自然地过好每一天
keyVocabulary.example: "Don't worry too much about the future; just take things one day at a time."
"We need to make a detailed plan before we go on our road trip."
"He goes to the gym and eats a lot of protein because he wants to gain weight."

keyVocabulary.translation: 鼓舞人心的 / 启发人的
keyVocabulary.example: "Listening to his success story was deeply inspiring."
"After I graduate from university and get a job, I plan to move out."
"You can go to the party as long as you finish your homework first."
"Riding the rollercoasters at the amusement park is my favorite thing to do."

grammarTips.title: Idiom: Take things one day at a time
grammarTips.description: Make sure to use the plural 'things'. This is a very natural native phrase used to comfort someone who is stressed about the future. It means to focus only on the present day without worrying about tomorrow.
grammarTips.incorrect: "I am so stressed, I will just take thing one day at a time."
grammarTips.correct: "I am so stressed, I will just take THINGS one day at a time."

grammarTips.title: Collocation: Keep a resolution
grammarTips.description: In English, we 'make' a resolution, and if we succeed, we 'keep' it. If we fail, we 'break' it. We do not 'do' a resolution.

grammarTips.title: Grammar: As long as
grammarTips.description: This is a great phrase to introduce a condition (similar to 'if'). It means 'on the condition that'.
grammarTips.incorrect: "I am happy if long as we are together."
grammarTips.correct: "I am happy AS LONG AS we are together."

grammarTips.title: Opposites: Gain weight vs. Lose weight
grammarTips.description: When talking about body weight, use 'gain' for going up and 'lose' for going down. Do not use 'add' or 'reduce'.
grammarTips.incorrect: "I want to add weight and my friend wants to reduce weight."
grammarTips.correct: "I want to GAIN weight and my friend wants to LOSE weight."

goals: Teach the difference between 'make', 'keep', and 'break' a resolution.
Ensure students correctly use 'gain weight' and 'lose weight' instead of add/reduce.
Practice using 'as long as' for conditionals in future plans.

phases.title: Phase 1: Resolutions & Planning (15-20 Minutes)
phases.description: Warm up by discussing New Year's resolutions and planning habits.
questions: What is your resolution for this year? Have you been able to keep it?
Are you a very strict planner, or do you prefer to take things one day at a time?

phases.title: Phase 2: Health & Independence (20-30 Minutes)
phases.description: Discuss health goals and big life steps like moving out.
questions: Who is the most inspiring person you know, and why?
At what age do people usually move out of their parents' house in your culture?

phases.title: Phase 3: Fun & Rewards (15-20 Minutes)
phases.description: Talk about balancing hard work with fun experiences, using 'as long as'.
questions: If you could go to any amusement park in the world right now, what is the first ride you would go on?
